success_surface: Successful mixed-library approach using pandas for complex row filtering combined with openpyxl for workbook preservation, employing safe row selection strategy rather than direct row deletion during iteration.
effective_behaviors: Employs hybrid library strategy: pandas for data filtering/logic, openpyxl for structural modifications and formatting preservation
Pre-validates spreadsheet structure by programmatically inspecting sheet names, columns, and sample values before executing transformations
Implements 'keep-list' pattern: identifies indices of rows to retain rather than calculating rows to delete, avoiding iterative deletion complications
Clears existing worksheet content entirely before writing filtered results to prevent stale data residue in non-deleted cells
Preserves header row by writing filtered data starting at row 2 rather than overwriting the first row
Logs transformation metrics (original count, filtered count, rows deleted) for verification and debugging transparency
Separates input and output file paths to guarantee original workbook integrity is maintained
evidence: Agent printed DataFrame shape, column names, and full content before implementing deletion logic
Agent computed rows_to_keep list explicitly (indices [2, 4, 5]) rather than marking deletions
Agent cleared worksheet with ws.delete_rows() loop before writing filtered data via cell assignment
Agent logged 'Original rows: 6', 'Filtered rows: 3', 'Rows deleted: 3' confirming expected outcome
Used separate input_path and output_path variables ensuring source file never modified in-place
Used openpyxl load_workbook() to retain formatting while pandas handled logical filtering

memory_items.title: Hybrid Processing Workflow
memory_items.lesson: Combine pandas for analytical operations with openpyxl for structure-aware modifications
memory_items.generalization: Load data into pandas for filtering/aggregation logic, but manage workbook object separately with openpyxl when formatting preservation or precise cell-level control is required

memory_items.title: Retention-Based Row Filtering
memory_items.lesson: Build lists of rows to KEEP rather than calculating rows to DELETE to avoid index shifting issues during iteration
memory_items.generalization: For conditional row removal tasks, compute the desired subset first, then reconstruct the worksheet from that subset rather than attempting to remove rows in place

memory_items.title: Pre-Modification Validation
memory_items.lesson: Always verify data structure before applying transformations to catch unexpected formats early
memory_items.generalization: Print sheet names, column headers, and representative data samples programmatically before implementing any modification logic

memory_items.title: Clean Slate Write Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Clear existing worksheet content completely before writing filtered results
memory_items.generalization: When replacing row data, delete all existing rows below header first, then write fresh data to ensure no residual values remain outside the new dataset bounds

memory_items.title: Transformation Logging
memory_items.lesson: Document before/after counts to validate filtering operations
memory_items.generalization: Record original dimension count, resulting dimension count, and number of affected rows to enable verification of correct execution

edits.content:
## 6. Advanced Row Operations
- **Retain-First Strategy**: For conditional row removal, identify and store indices of rows to KEEP rather than rows to DELETE. Reconstruct the worksheet from the retained subset to avoid index-shifting complications during iteration.
- **Pre-Filter Validation**: Programmatically inspect sheet structure (names, columns, sample values) before implementing filtering logic to catch unexpected data formats early.
- **Clean Slate Writing**: When replacing filtered data, clear all existing worksheet rows above max_row first, then write the filtered dataset starting at row 2 (header preserved) to eliminate stale data residue.
- **Transformation Metrics**: Log original row count, filtered row count, and rows affected in execution output to enable immediate verification of correct filtering behavior.
